source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the figure, equilateral hexagon $ABCDEF$ has three nonadjacent acute interior angles that each measure $30^\circ$. The enclosed area of the hexagon is $6\sqrt{3}$. What is the perimeter of the hexagon?
<image1>

image_dependency: geometry depends on arrangement of three nonadjacent acute angles in an equilateral hexagon
multi_step: requires combining geometric angle/configuration information with area-to-side-length reasoning
target asks for perimeter but given quantity is area, so at least one intermediate derivation is needed
multiple-choice geometry item with nontrivial structural constraints suggests multi-step solving
verifiability: answer is a specific short_text numeric expression: $12\sqrt{3}$
choices include a matching option, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
asset registry passed with question and answer present, so the sample is structurally easy to validate

risk_flags: figure-specific geometric configuration may not be fully reconstructable from text alone

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with question, answer, and primary image all present
high verifiability and strong multi-step/image-dependency signals make the sample valuable
the figure-specific risk is mitigated because the image asset exists, so core semantics are recoverable
